commit.message:
fix(sqlite): apply libsql pragmas per connection

Move shared SQLite pragma handling into stakpak-shared, apply per-connection busy timeout/synchronous settings across the watch, API, and gateway stores, verify WAL startup behavior with contention regression tests, and replace secret-like test literals so the branch can pass push protection.

commit.message: fix(test): set executable permission on check script in schedule_existing_check_script_is_accepted
fix(autopilot): improve deployment preflight diagnostics

fix(autopilot): handle ERANGE retry and validate check script is executable file

- Retry getpwuid_r with larger buffer (up to 64KB) on ERANGE error
- Validate check script is a file (not directory)
- Validate check script has executable permission on Unix systems

Addresses reviewer feedback on PR #641

feat: auto-discover predefined slash commands from libs/api/src/commands/

Move /claw and /review prompts from hardcoded include_str! entries in
the TUI crate to .md files in libs/api/src/commands/, embedded at
compile time via include_dir. Any .md file added to that directory
automatically appears as a slash command in the TUI dropdown.

- Add libs/api/src/commands.rs with load_predefined_commands(), version
  parsing (highest .vN wins), and front-matter extraction
- Move claw.v1.md and review.v1.md from prompts/ to commands/ with
  YAML front matter for descriptions
- Remove hardcoded /claw and /review entries from TUI commands list
- Wire predefined commands into get_helper_commands() with dedup
  against built-in and custom commands (BuiltIn > Predefined > Custom)
style: Reformat tool result message pushing for improved readability.

fix(ask-user): display error immediately and retry via LLM on parse failure

Previously, when ask_user tool args failed to parse or had empty questions,
the error was added to messages but not rendered in the TUI (only showing a
checkmark until user typed). Also, the error path fell through into normal
tool execution instead of sending the error back to the LLM for retry.

Now both error paths send ToolResult events to the TUI for immediate display
and use `continue` to skip tool execution, letting the LLM see the error and
retry with corrected arguments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
fix(autopilot): validate tilde check paths and document service-safe absolute paths

commit.message:
refactor(tui): add BuiltInWithPrompt variant for generic prompt-based commands

Addresses PR #640 review feedback: introduce CommandSource::BuiltInWithPrompt
so /claw and /review are handled generically by the _ => fallback in
execute_command() instead of requiring dedicated match arms.

- Add BuiltInWithPrompt { prompt_content } variant to CommandSource enum
- Move /review prompt to libs/api/src/prompts/review.v1.md (include_str!)
- /claw and /review now use BuiltInWithPrompt in commands_to_helper_commands()
- Remove bespoke /claw and /review match arms from execute_command()
- Unified _ => fallback handles BuiltInWithPrompt, Custom, and {input}
  placeholder interpolation generically
- Tab autocomplete adds trailing space for prompts containing {input}
- Defense-in-depth catches BuiltInWithPrompt commands with args

feat(tui): add custom slash commands and /review command

- Load user-defined slash commands from .stakpak/commands/ (project) and
  ~/.stakpak/commands/ (global) markdown files with optional YAML front matter
- Hot-reload custom commands when '/' dropdown opens
- Custom commands autocomplete on Enter/Tab, accept optional extra text
- Show [custom] prefix in dropdown to distinguish from built-in commands
- Fix build_review_command: raw string for inner quotes, proper return, accept input
- Wire /review as built-in command with optional commit/branch/PR argument
- Change HelperCommand from &'static str to String with CommandSource enum
- Defense-in-depth handles custom commands with arguments
- 12 unit tests covering front matter parsing, validation, and edge cases

commit.message:
fix(test): correct validate_profile_reference_surfaces_config_load_errors test

The test expected a missing config file to produce an error, but
AppConfig::load_config_file() returns Ok(ConfigFile::with_default_profile())
on NotFound — which includes a 'default' profile. So
validate_profile_reference("default", ...) always succeeded, causing the
assertion to fail.

Fix: write invalid TOML to the temp path so load_config_file actually
hits a parse error. Also clean up the temp file after the test.

commit.message:
fix: remap unknown provider entries in recent_models to stakpak

When recent_models contains entries with unrecognized provider prefixes
(e.g., fireworks-ai/accounts/fireworks/models/glm-5), get_default_model()
would use that provider directly, causing 'Provider not found' errors.

The migration now detects entries whose provider is not a known cloud
provider, not stakpak, and not explicitly configured by the user, and
re-maps them to stakpak/{short_name} so they route correctly through
the Stakpak API.
chore: bump version to 0.3.66

commit.message:
fix: improve channel tool execution and schedule trigger formatting

Tool execution messages:
- Strip MCP/namespace prefixes from tool names (stakpak__run_command → run_command)
- Show tool argument previews (commands, file paths, diffs) in auto-approve mode
- Use numbered bold labels (e.g. **1 · run_command**) for visual separation
- Add render_running_tools_summary() matching approval prompt style

Schedule trigger messages:
- Replace generic '⚙️ [name] Schedule fired' with contextual formats
- Vary emoji by trigger type: 🕐 scheduled, ⚠️ check failed, ⏩ check passed, 👤 manual
- Bold schedule name for readability
- Include check exit code when available
- Thread manual vs cron trigger distinction through handle_schedule_event

Approval prompt:
- Switch from numbered markdown lists to inline numbered bold labels
- Fixes Slack resetting list counters when code blocks appear between items
- Use standard markdown **bold** instead of Slack mrkdwn *bold*

commit.message: Fmt

fix: prevent duplicate text when sending markdown tables to Slack

When a message contains a markdown table but no other block content,
the Slack message was sent with blocks=None and the full fallback text
in the 'text' field. Slack renders 'text' as the visible message body
when blocks is absent, causing the raw table data to appear above the
formatted table attachment.

Two fixes:
1. In send_with_receipt(), send empty 'text' when blocks is None but
   attachments are present (table-only messages). This prevents Slack
   from rendering the fallback as visible content.

2. In generate_fallback_text(), skip table content entirely and emit
   a '[table]' placeholder instead. Previously, all table cell text
   events were concatenated without separators, producing unreadable
   output like 'IDNameAge1Alice30' in notifications.

Added 2 tests for the new fallback behavior.

fix: improve tool approval rendering for Slack/Discord

Add dedicated preview renderers for complex tool calls that previously
fell through to the generic fallback, producing ugly raw key=value dumps.

New dedicated renderers:
- dynamic_subagent_task: shows description (with sandbox badge), tool
  list, and instructions in a code block
- search_docs: shows keywords as comma-separated inline code
- ask_user: shows numbered question labels

Improved generic fallback for multi-param tools:
- Builds compact dot-separated summary instead of dumping first key=value
- Type-aware formatting (short strings inline, long strings truncated,
  arrays as counts, booleans/numbers as-is, objects as key counts)
- Caps at 4 params with overflow indicator

Before (dynamic_subagent_task):
  context=`- User is on macOS (machine: 42.local)...`

After:
  *Enumerate S3 buckets* (🛡 sandboxed)
  tools: `view`, `run_command`, `search_docs`
  ```
  List all S3 buckets in the account.
  Include regions and sizes.
  ```

refactor: deduplicate normalize_optional_string into shared utils

- Extract the three identical private normalize_optional_string
  implementations (autopilot.rs, profile_resolver.rs, gateway/config.rs)
  into a single public function in libs/shared/src/utils.rs.

- Remove duplicate NoopRunOverrideResolver from gateway/runtime.rs;
  expose noop_run_override_resolver() constructor from dispatcher.rs
  and reuse it in DispatcherProfileOverrides::none().

- Add unit test for normalize_optional_string covering trim, empty,
  and None cases.

feat(overrides): consolidate RunOverrides to shared crate and add profile-based resolution

- Move RunOverrides and AutoApproveOverride from server::types and
  gateway::client to libs/shared/models/overrides.rs as single source of
  truth, with pub use re-exports preserving existing API surface.

- Add system_prompt and max_turns fields to ProfileConfig and AppConfig,
  with validation (max_turns 1-256, system_prompt ≤32KB) enforced at
  profile load time via ProfileConfig::validate().

- Extract resolve_profile_run_overrides into cli/src/config/profile_resolver.rs,
  removing the boot-profile short-circuit so schedule/channel overrides
  resolve the named profile regardless of whether it matches the boot profile.

- Introduce RunOverrideResolver trait in gateway dispatcher for lazy
  profile-to-overrides resolution at run time instead of eager config
  mutation at startup. Replaces resolve_gateway_channel_profiles with
  DispatcherProfileOverrides passed through Gateway::new_with_profile_overrides.

- Store resolved approval mode/allowlist in ActiveRun so approval semantics
  are frozen for the lifetime of a run, not re-resolved on each callback.

- Add --profile flag to autopilot schedule add and channel add commands,
  with validation that referenced profiles exist in config.toml.

- Add deprecation warnings for inline model/auto_approve channel fields
  when profile is set, surfaced in both autopilot doctor and foreground
  runtime startup.

- Add profiles_map() and check_deprecations() to gateway ChannelConfigs
  for profile discovery and migration guidance.
Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/stakpak/agent into feat/per-request-run-overrides

docs: add sandbox mode configuration to autopilot system prompt

Documents how to configure sandbox lifecycle mode in autopilot.toml:
- persistent (default): single container reused across sessions
- ephemeral: new container per session for maximum isolation

Also notes per-schedule --sandbox flag for individual schedules.
chore: bump version to 0.3.64

commit.message: Fmt

fix(autopilot): show image pull progress and spinner during stakpak up

When persistent sandbox mode is configured, `stakpak up` now:

1. Pre-pulls the sandbox image before starting the service if it's not
   cached locally. The pull runs with inherited stdout/stderr so Docker's
   native progress bars (layer downloads, extraction) are visible to the
   user instead of a silent wait.

2. Uses the correct platform (linux/amd64) matching what warden hardcodes
   for all container operations. Previously a bare `docker pull` on Apple
   Silicon would fail with 'no matching manifest for linux/arm64/v8'.

3. Replaces the static 'Waiting for autopilot to be ready...' message
   with an animated spinner showing the current phase and elapsed time:
   - 'Starting server...' while the health endpoint is unreachable
   - 'Starting sandbox container...' once the server is up but sandbox
     isn't healthy yet

New shared utilities in container module:
- WARDEN_PLATFORM const ('linux/amd64')
- warden_image_exists_locally() - platform-aware local image check
- pull_warden_image() - platform-aware pull with visible progress
chore: bump version to 0.3.63

Merge pull request #629 from stakpak/fix/ask-user-experience

commit.message:
fix: use read_timeout instead of total timeout for HTTP client

Replace .timeout(300s) with .read_timeout(300s) + .connect_timeout(30s)
in create_platform_tls_client(). The total timeout was killing SSE
streams that run longer than 5 minutes during long tool calls, causing
'Transport error: TimedOut' failures. read_timeout only fires when no
data arrives for the given duration (idle detection), which is the
correct behavior for streaming connections.
fix: persist recent_models migration to disk and prevent config model from being truncated

Add test fields to MockAgentConfig

Added `boot_profile` and `config_path` fields to the `MockAgentConfig`
struct within the test module for
`cli/src/commands/watch/commands/run.rs`. These fields are necessary for
more comprehensive testing scenarios.

fix: migrate old-format recent_models and ensure config model is included

- Add migrate_recent_models() that normalizes bare model names (e.g., 'glm-4.6')
  by prefixing them with the config model's provider, and drops bare entries that
  duplicate an existing prefixed entry (e.g., 'claude-sonnet-4-6' is dropped when
  'stakpak/claude-sonnet-4-6' already exists)
- Ensure the config model field (e.g., 'z/glm-4.6') is always present in
  recent_models so it appears in the model switcher
- Run migration at profile load time alongside existing migrations
Merge branch 'main' of https://github.com/stakpak/agent into feat/per-request-run-overrides

feat: normalize recent_models format and default to last used model

- Store recent_models in 'provider/short_name' format (e.g., 'stakpak/glm-5')
  instead of full upstream paths like 'fireworks-ai/accounts/fireworks/models/glm-5'
- Add format_recent_model_id() helper in both CLI (profile.rs) and TUI (popup.rs)
  to normalize model IDs by extracting the last path segment as the short name
- Update model_switcher.rs to match available_models against normalized recent IDs
- Update ensure_custom_models_in_available to compare using normalized format
- Default to recent_models[0] when no --model flag is specified, so re-opening
  stakpak continues with the last model you were using
- Priority chain is now: --model flag > recent_models[0] > config model > claude-opus-4-5
chore: bump version to 0.3.62

Merge pull request #626 from stakpak/fix/skip-theme-detection-async-mode

fix: skip terminal theme detection in async mode

commit.message:
fix: skip terminal theme detection in async mode

terminal_light::luma() hangs indefinitely when called from async
subagent processes. The library acquires io::stdout().lock(), writes
an OSC escape sequence, then blocks on select() waiting for a terminal
response on /dev/tty. When the subagent inherits the parent's TTY but
the parent TUI is actively reading input, the response never arrives
and the process deadlocks at startup.

This prevented async subagents from ever reaching the agent loop,
making pause-on-approval appear broken (subagents showed as perpetually
'Running' instead of 'Paused').

Fix: force Theme::Dark in async/print mode since headless processes
don't need terminal theme detection.

fix: resolve short model names for Stakpak provider and display model in session usage

Unknown model names passed via --model (e.g., 'GLM-5') were defaulting to
the Anthropic provider and failing with a 404. This fixes two issues:

1. Default unknown unprefixed models to Stakpak (when a Stakpak key exists)
   instead of hardcoding Anthropic as the fallback provider.

2. Resolve short model names against the Stakpak /v1/models catalog after
   client creation, matching by last ID segment or display name to get the
   full prefixed model ID the API requires.

Also adds the model name (provider/name) to the Session Usage output in
both async and interactive modes.

fix: use provider-agnostic label for OpenAI-compatible stream errors

The OpenAI stream parser is also used for custom OpenAI-compatible
providers (Ollama, etc.), so the fallback label should not reference
OpenAI specifically.

fix: detect provider error responses in unparseable stream chunks

When an upstream provider (Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, etc.) is down or
overloaded, it may send an error JSON instead of a normal streaming
chunk. Previously, all 5 stream parsers failed with a cryptic serde
error like 'missing field `id` at line 1 column 80'.

Add Error::from_unparseable_chunk() — a reusable helper that:
1. Checks if the raw data is a provider error JSON
   ({"error": {"type": "...", "message": "..."}})
2. Returns a meaningful ProviderError if so
3. Falls back to a 200-char preview of the raw data for debugging

All 5 stream parsers now use this helper:
- Stakpak, OpenAI, Anthropic, Bedrock, Gemini

fix(watch): inject runtime gateway auth token into scheduler config

The scheduler reads NotificationConfig.gateway_token from autopilot.toml,
but the gateway auth token is generated fresh each `stakpak up` and never
persisted to disk. This caused 401 Unauthorized on every POST
/v1/gateway/send call (notifications and interactive schedule sends),
while inbound DMs still worked because they bypass the HTTP API.

Add ScheduleConfig::apply_runtime_gateway_credentials() which patches
the in-memory notification config with the ephemeral runtime token.
Called after initial config load and after every hot-reload so the
token is never lost when the config is re-read from disk.

- Trim whitespace-only tokens (symmetric with URL handling)
- Preserve user-configured external gateway URLs
- No-op when [notifications] section is absent
- Thread &AgentServerConnection through trigger_config_reload

Tests: 12 unit tests for apply_runtime_gateway_credentials edge cases,
5 integration tests verifying token survives reloads (including
consecutive reloads, stale disk tokens, and external URL preservation).
refactor: Reorder imports in message and misc handlers and reformat a comment.

docs(slack): reference app manifest in system prompt, CLI help, and init flow

- Add 'Slack App Setup' section to system_prompt.v1.md with the full
  manifest YAML embedded inline and an 8-step walkthrough so the agent
  always recommends the manifest-based approach over manual scope config
- Update channel add --help to recommend manifest-first setup with a
  direct GitHub link, relabeling manual steps as fallback
- Add manifest URL hint during interactive first-run setup when no
  channel env vars are detected
- Add missing 'Interactivity & Shortcuts → enable' step to manual setup
fix: Allow backend events to bypass popup interception to maintain TUI message flow.

feat(config): update default model to claude-opus-4-6

- Update fallback model in AppConfig::resolve_model()
- Update DEFAULT_MODEL constant in config_templates

feat: harden persistent sandbox — fail explicitly, no silent fallbacks

## Breaking Changes

- Server startup now hard-fails when persistent sandbox cannot be spawned
  instead of silently falling back to no sandbox. This ensures the server
  never operates without the required sandbox.

## Sandbox Reliability

- Add circuit breaker to health monitor: after 5 failed respawn attempts,
  the monitor gives up and marks the sandbox as permanently failed instead
  of retrying indefinitely.
- Add `total_respawn_attempts` to `SandboxHealth` for observability.
- Session actor now explicitly rejects sessions when sandbox mode is
  'persistent' but no persistent sandbox is available, instead of silently
  falling through to ephemeral mode.
- Add warning log when killing persistent sandbox (in-flight sessions will fail).
- Add TODO documenting the TOCTOU port race in `find_free_port()`.

## UX Improvements

- Readiness check (`stakpak up`) now waits for sandbox health when
  persistent mode is configured, with actionable troubleshooting output
  on timeout.
- `stakpak autopilot status` now shows sandbox column in schedules table.
- `stakpak autopilot schedule list` now shows sandbox column.
- `stakpak autopilot schedule history` now shows sandbox column per run.
- `stakpak autopilot schedule show` now shows sandbox field in detail view.
- Preserve `auto_approve_all` from config file when CLI flag is at default.
- More actionable error messages for sandbox spawn failures.

Merge pull request #618 from stakpak/fix/ask-user-approval-gap

fix: ask_user tool hangs and shows unnecessary approval bar

commit.message:
fix: auto-approve ask_user tool at all queue sites and fix queue stall after response

ask_user tools that were not the first tool in a batch went through the
approval bar, creating an async gap where the user saw a dead 'Ask User'
placeholder and Enter did nothing. Additionally, the AskUserResponse handler
did not dispatch the next tool from the queue, causing the session to hang
when ask_user was followed by other tools.

- Extract send_next_tool_from_queue() helper that auto-approves ask_user
  by sending ShowAskUserPopup directly (bypasses approval bar)
- Apply at all 9 queue-processing sites (previously only 1 had ask_user
  handling), including both session resume paths
- Add next-tool dispatch in AskUserResponse handler (fixes queue stall)
- Clean up stale pending border block when ShowAskUserPopup arrives

fix: make slash command filtering synchronous to fix broken /commands in external terminals

Slash command filtering was done asynchronously via the file_search_worker,
creating a race condition between synchronous state updates (show_helper_dropdown)
and async filter results arriving later with stale data. In external terminals
(iTerm2, Warp, etc.) the timing difference made the race visible — unfiltered
commands, dropdown not appearing, and /commands sent as user messages.

- Add filter_helpers_sync() for instant slash command filtering (~15 items)
- Call it from handle_input_changed, handle_input_backspace, handle_paste,
  and handle_input_delete_word
- Stop poll_file_search_results() from overwriting filtered_helpers (async
  worker now only handles @ file completion)
- Initialize filtered_helpers to empty Vec instead of all helpers
- Add defense-in-depth: exact slash command matches execute on submit even
  if dropdown state is out of sync (prevents /model sent as user message)
- Fix trim() inconsistency and remove special-case for input == "/"
  across helper_dropdown.rs and navigation.rs
chore: bump version to 0.3.61

Refactor: Use channel profile for overridden channels

This commit makes a few changes to how channel overrides are handled:

- When applying a profile to a channel, the original profile name is now
  preserved in memory to prevent accidental configuration loss if the
  `GatewayConfig` is persisted later.
- The `build_run_overrides` function in `Dispatcher` no longer takes
  `effective_model` as an argument, simplifying its signature and logic.
  The model resolution is now handled separately.
- The `max_turns` and `system_prompt` overrides in
  `SessionMessageRequest` are now validated to be within acceptable
  ranges, with `max_turns` clamped between 1 and 256, and
  `system_prompt` length checked against a maximum of 32KB.

These changes improve the robustness and clarity of channel
configuration and override handling.

feat(server): add per-request run overrides for model, approval, and prompt

- Add RunOverrides and RunConfig types to stakpak-server with
  AutoApproveOverride (mode string or explicit allowlist)
- Parse 'overrides' field on POST /v1/sessions/{id}/messages and
  resolve tool approval policy, system prompt, model, and max turns
- Refactor session_actor to accept RunConfig instead of bare model,
  threading overrides through inference and approval pipelines
- Add per-channel override fields (model, auto_approve, profile) to
  gateway TelegramConfig, DiscordConfig, and SlackConfig
- Implement ChannelOverrides and overrides_map() for gateway config,
  with normalization of empty strings and whitespace-only values
- Wire channel-aware model/approval resolution in Dispatcher with
  resolve_effective_model(), resolve_channel_approval(), and
  build_run_overrides() flowing overrides to server API calls
- Add resolve_schedule_profile_overrides() in watch for per-schedule
  profile-based model and allowed_tools resolution
- Add boot_profile and config_path to AgentServerConnection for
  profile resolution at schedule execution time
- Update OpenAPI docs with RunOverridesDoc and AutoApproveOverrideDoc
- Add comprehensive tests across all layers: RunOverrides::is_empty(),
  channel overrides map, tool approval override resolution, schedule
  profile overrides, and dispatcher channel override behavior

commit.message:
Format unknown tool args with priority keys

Iterate through a predefined list of common keys to find the most
relevant argument for displaying a preview of an unknown tool's
arguments. Truncate the value to 120 characters for readability.

releases.name: v0.3.67
releases.body:
## 0.3.67
Released on 2026-03-10.

### Bug Fixes

- Remap unknown provider entries in recent_models to stakpak
- Correct validate_profile_reference_surfaces_config_load_errors test
- Validate tilde check paths and document service-safe absolute paths
- Handle ERANGE retry and validate check script is executable file
- Set executable permission on check script in schedule_existing_check_script_is_accepted
- Improve deployment preflight diagnostics
- Display error immediately and retry via LLM on parse failure
- Apply libsql pragmas per connection

### Code Style

- Reformat tool result message pushing for improved readability.

### Features

- Add per-request run overrides for model, approval, and prompt
- Consolidate RunOverrides to shared crate and add profile-based resolution
- Add custom slash commands and /review command
- Auto-discover predefined slash commands from libs/api/src/commands/

### Maintenance

- Bump version to 0.3.67

### Refactor

- Use channel profile for overridden channels

### Refactoring

- Deduplicate normalize_optional_string into shared utils
- Add BuiltInWithPrompt variant for generic prompt-based commands

releases.body:

### Maintenance

### Bug Fixes

### Features

- Dynamically truncate the session ID to fit available display width and use a flexible copy status suffix.

### Maintenance

releases.name: v0.3.64

## 0.3.64
Released on 2026-02-27.

### Bug Fixes

- Resolve short model names for Stakpak provider and display model in session usage
- Migrate old-format recent_models and ensure config model is included
- Persist recent_models migration to disk and prevent config model from being truncated
- Show image pull progress and spinner during stakpak up

### Features

- Normalize recent_models format and default to last used model

### Maintenance

- Bump version to 0.3.64

releases.name: v0.3.63
releases.body:
## 0.3.63
Released on 2026-02-26.

### Bug Fixes

- Use read_timeout instead of total timeout for HTTP client
- Allow backend events to bypass popup interception to maintain TUI message flow.

### Documentation

- Fix sandbox mode default references to match Persistent

### Features

- Harden persistent sandbox — fail explicitly, no silent fallbacks
- Improve "Ask User" block responsiveness by forcing cache invalidation and refining keybinding behavior to always navigate options with arrow keys.

### Maintenance

- Bump version to 0.3.63

### Refactoring

- Refactor
- Reorder imports in message and misc handlers and reformat a comment.

readme_html:
:star: Help us reach more developers and grow the Stakpak community. Star this repo!

Try Stakpak Now

curl -sSL https://stakpak.dev/install.sh | sh # install Stakpak
stakpak init # understand your apps and tech stack
stakpak autopilot up # start the autonomous agent, running 24/7 in the background

For more installation options...

You can't trust most AI agents with your DevOps. One mistake, and your production is toast. Stakpak is built different:

  • Secret Substitution - The LLM works with your credentials without ever seeing them
  • Warden Guardrails - Network-level policies block destructive operations before they run
  • DevOps Playbooks Baked-in - Curated library of DevOps knowledge in Stakpak Rulebooks

Generate infrastructure code, debug Kubernetes, configure CI/CD, automate deployments, without giving an LLM the keys to production.

🤖 Autopilot (24/7 Autonomous Runtime)

Use the new lifecycle aliases for one-command setup/start/stop:

stakpak up        # alias for: stakpak autopilot up
stakpak down      # alias for: stakpak autopilot down

You can also use the canonical subcommands:

stakpak autopilot up
stakpak autopilot status
stakpak autopilot logs
stakpak autopilot down
stakpak autopilot doctor

Autopilot prerequisites

Before running autopilot on a remote VM:

  • Docker must be installed and accessible to the current user
  • 2GB+ RAM is recommended for reliable autopilot + sandbox runs
  • Swap is strongly recommended on small Linux hosts
  • Linux user services may require linger to survive logout

stakpak up now runs preflight checks before startup, and stakpak autopilot doctor can be used as a deployment-readiness check before first boot:

stakpak autopilot doctor
stakpak up

See also: cli/README.md

Unified configuration (profiles + autopilot wiring)

  • ~/.stakpak/config.toml: profile behavior (model, allowed_tools, auto_approve, system_prompt, max_turns, provider credentials)
  • ~/.stakpak/autopilot.toml: runtime wiring (schedules, channels, service/server settings)

Use profile = "name" on schedules/channels and keep behavior inside profile definitions.

# schedule profile
stakpak autopilot schedule add health --cron '*/5 * * * *' --prompt 'Check health' --profile monitoring

# channel profile
stakpak autopilot channel add slack --bot-token "$SLACK_BOT_TOKEN" --app-token "$SLACK_APP_TOKEN" --profile ops

Full setup guide: cli/README.md

🔒 Security Hardened

  • Mutual TLS (mTLS) - End-to-end encrypted MCP
  • Dynamic Secret Substitution - AI can read/write/compare secrets without seeing actual values
  • Secure Password Generation - Generate cryptographically secure passwords with configurable complexity
  • Privacy Mode - Redacts sensitive data like IP addresses and AWS account IDs

🛠️ Built for DevOps Work

  • Asynchronous Task Management - Run background commands like port forwarding and servers with proper tracking and cancellation
  • Real-time Progress Streaming - Long-running processes (Docker builds, deployments) stream progress updates in real-time
  • Infrastructure Code Indexing - Automatic local indexing and semantic search for Terraform, Kubernetes, Dockerfile, and GitHub Actions
  • Documentation Research Agent - Built-in web search for technical documentation, cloud providers, and development frameworks
  • Subagents - Specialized research agents for code exploration and sandboxed analysis with different tool access levels (enabled with --enable-subagents flag)
  • Bulk Message Approval - Approve multiple tool calls at once for efficient workflow execution
  • Reversible File Operations - All file modifications are automatically backed up with recovery capabilities

🧠 Adaptive Intelligence

  • Rule Books - Customize agent behavior with internal standard operating procedures, playbooks, and organizational policies
  • Persistent Knowledge - Agent learns from interactions, remembers incidents, resources, and environment details to adapt to your workflow

Installation

All installation options (Linux, MacOs, Windows)

Check the docs

Homebrew (Linux & MacOS)

brew tap stakpak/stakpak
brew install stakpak

To update it you can use

brew update
brew upgrade stakpak

Binary Release

Download the latest binary for your platform from our GitHub Releases.

Docker

This image includes the most popular CLI tools the agent might need for everyday DevOps tasks like docker, kubectl, aws cli, gcloud, azure cli, and more.

docker pull ghcr.io/stakpak/agent:latest

Usage

You can use your own Anthropic or OpenAI API keys, custom OpenAI compatible endpoint, or a Stakpak API key.

Option A: Running with a Stakpak API Key (no card required)

Just run stakpak and follow the instructions which will create a new API key for you.

stakpak

Brave users may encounter issues with automatic redirects to localhost ports during the API key creation flow. If this happens to you:

Copy your new key from the browser paste it in your terminal

Non-interactive setup (CI/scripts)

stakpak auth login --api-key $STAKPAK_API_KEY

Or set the environment variable

export STAKPAK_API_KEY=<mykey>

View current account (Optional)

stakpak account

Option B: Running Without a Stakpak API Key

Non-interactive setup (CI/scripts)

# Anthropic
stakpak auth login --provider anthropic --api-key $ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

# OpenAI
stakpak auth login --provider openai --api-key $OPENAI_API_KEY

# Gemini
stakpak auth login --provider gemini --api-key $GEMINI_API_KEY

Manual configuration

Create ~/.stakpak/config.toml with one of these configurations:

Option 1: Bring Your Own Keys (BYOK) - Use your Anthropic/OpenAI API keys:

[profiles.byok]
provider = "local"

# Unified model preference field
model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5"

# Built-in providers - credentials can also be set via environment variables
# (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GEMINI_API_KEY)
[profiles.byok.providers.anthropic]
type = "anthropic"
api_key = "sk-ant-..."

[profiles.byok.providers.openai]
type = "openai"
api_key = "sk-..."

[profiles.byok.providers.gemini]
type = "gemini"
api_key = "..."

[settings]

Option 2: Bring Your Own LLM - Use a local OpenAI-compatible endpoint (e.g. Ollama, LM Studio):

[profiles.offline]
provider = "local"

# Custom provider models use the format: provider_key/model_name
model = "offline/qwen/qwen3-coder-30b"

# The provider key "offline" becomes the model prefix
[profiles.offline.providers.offline]
type = "custom"
api_endpoint = "http://localhost:11434/v1"
# api_key is optional for local providers

[settings]

Option 3: Mix Built-in and Custom Providers:

[profiles.hybrid]
provider = "local"

# Unified model field (provider-prefixed)
model = "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5"

[profiles.hybrid.providers.anthropic]
type = "anthropic"
# Uses ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var

[profiles.hybrid.providers.offline]
type = "custom"
api_endpoint = "http://localhost:11434/v1"

[settings]

Then run with your profile:

stakpak --profile byok
# or
stakpak --profile offline
# or
stakpak --profile hybrid

Start Stakpak Agent TUI

# Open the TUI
stakpak
# Resume execution from a checkpoint
stakpak -c <checkpoint-id>

Start Stakpak Agent TUI with Docker

docker run -it --entrypoint stakpak ghcr.io/stakpak/agent:latest
# for containerization tasks (you need to mount the Docker socket)
docker run -it \
   -v "/var/run/docker.sock":"/var/run/docker.sock" \
   -v "{your app path}":"/agent/" \
   --entrypoint stakpak ghcr.io/stakpak/agent:latest

MCP Modes

You can use Stakpak as a secure MCP proxy or expose its security-hardened tools through an MCP server.

MCT Server Tools

  • Local Mode (--tool-mode local) - File operations and command execution only (no API key required)
  • Remote Mode (--tool-mode remote) - AI-powered code generation and search tools (API key required)
  • Combined Mode (--tool-mode combined) - Both local and remote tools (default, API key required)

Start MCP Server

# Local tools only (no API key required, mTLS enabled by default)
stakpak mcp start --tool-mode local

# Remote tools only (AI tools optimized for DevOps)
stakpak mcp start --tool-mode remote

# Combined mode (default - all tools with full security)
stakpak mcp start

# Disable mTLS (NOT recommended for production)
stakpak mcp start --disable-mcp-mtls

Additional flags for the MCP server:

  • --disable-secret-redaction – not recommended; prints secrets in plaintext to the console
  • --privacy-mode – redacts additional private data like IP addresses and AWS account IDs
  • --enable-slack-tools – enables experimental Slack tools

MCP Proxy Server

Stakpak also includes an MCP proxy server that can multiplex connections to multiple upstream MCP servers using a configuration file.

# Start MCP proxy with automatic config discovery
stakpak mcp proxy

# Start MCP proxy with explicit config file
stakpak mcp proxy --config-file ~/.stakpak/mcp.toml

# Disable secret redaction (NOT recommended – secrets will be printed in logs)
stakpak mcp proxy --disable-secret-redaction

# Enable privacy mode to redact IPs, account IDs, etc.
stakpak mcp proxy --privacy-mode

Agent Client Protocol (ACP)

ACP is a standardized protocol that enables AI agents to integrate directly with code editors like Zed, providing seamless AI-powered development assistance.

What ACP Offers with Stakpak

  • Real-time AI Chat - Natural language conversations with context-aware AI assistance
  • Live Code Analysis - AI can read, understand, and modify your codebase in real-time
  • Tool Execution - AI can run commands, edit files, search code, and perform development tasks
  • Session Persistence - Maintains conversation context across editor sessions
  • Streaming Responses - Real-time AI responses with live progress updates
  • Agent Plans - Visual task breakdown and progress tracking

Installation & Setup

  1. Install Stakpak (if not already installed)
  2. Configure Zed Editor - Add to ~/.config/zed/settings.json:
{
  "agent_servers": {
    "Stakpak": {
      "command": "stakpak",
      "args": ["acp"],
      "env": {}
    }
  }
}
  1. Start ACP Agent:
stakpak acp
  1. Use in Zed - Click Assistant (✨) → + → New stakpak thread

Rulebook Management

Manage your standard operating procedures (SOPs), playbooks, and runbooks with Stakpak Rulebooks. Rulebooks customize agent behavior and provide context-specific guidance.

# List all rulebooks
stakpak rulebooks get
# or use the short alias
stakpak rb get

# Get a specific rulebook
stakpak rb get stakpak://my-org/deployment-guide.md

# Create or update a rulebook from a markdown file
stakpak rb apply my-rulebook.md

# Delete a rulebook
stakpak rb delete stakpak://my-org/old-guide.md

Rulebook Format

Rulebooks are markdown files with YAML frontmatter:

---
uri: stakpak://my-org/deployment-guide.md
description: Standard deployment procedures for production
tags:
  - deployment
  - production
  - sop
---

# Deployment Guide

Your deployment procedures and guidelines here...

Shell Mode

Execute system commands explicitly from the input bar.

Check the Shell Mode docs for details on background vs foreground execution.
